TORNADO SPUN UP 6 WNW FARIBAULT, TRACKED ACROSS ROBERDS LAKE AND I-35, BEFORE ENDING 5 N FARIBAULT. THE MOST EXTENSIVE DAMAGE OCCURRED ON THE EAST SIDE OF ROBERDS LAKE WHERE NUMEROUS HOMES, BOATS, AND TREES WERE DAMAGED. WITNESS STATED THE WATER WAS BEING BLOWN WESTWARD BEFORE QUICKLY RETURNING TO THE EAST AS THE TORNADO PASSED. HIGH TENSION WIRES WERE DOWN ALONG I-35.

SEVERE THUNDERSTORM WINDS OCCURRED FROM ABOUT 4 NW OF MORRISTOWN EAST TO THE SOUTHERN SIDE OF THE NORTHWEST FARIBAULT TORNADO, (INCLUDING THE FARIBAULT AIRPORT AREA), TO CANNON FALLS. WINDS WERE MOST EXTREME NEAR THE AIRPORT WITH ESTIMATED SPEEDS AROUND 110 MPH. TORNADOES MAY HAVE SPUN UP WITHIN THIS SWATH, BUT ADDITIONAL INFORMATION IS NEEDED.
CALLED IN TO REPORT TREES DOWN AND UPROOTED. A ROOF WAS BLOWN OFF A SHED AND THERE ARE ROAD BLOCKAGES. RADAR CONFIRMED TORNADO.
